mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month change in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income security and weaker earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 5.7 as of July 2008
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment-rate increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses below 2.25% after a large prior decline, the cut reflects recession and credit-stress information rather than pure discount-rate relief, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 2.25% after declining by more than 1.0pp over 12 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 2.01 as of July 2008
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS downside-threshold episodes generate average 20-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional average with t-test p < 0.05, or if the signal has negative-hit-rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and rise in unemployment signals a housing-led demand contraction spilling into labor income, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% year over year while UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 1066 thousand units as of June 2008
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-down and UNRATE-up condition is not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales indicates resilient nominal consumption and near-term revenue support for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 384631 million dollars as of July 2008
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS 3-month momentum is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or if the estimated return spread versus negative momentum is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals falling capacity utilization and weaker cyclicals earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 111.8419 as of July 2008
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside-crossing episodes do not produce lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered episodes, or if the return difference has the wrong sign in two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: High CPI inflation combined with a low federal funds rate signals negative real policy rates and limited ability to ease against growth shocks, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 4% while FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 219.181 as of July 2008
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 2.01 as of July 2008
failure_condition: Reject if high-CPI and low-FEDFUNDS joint episodes have positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or outperform episodes with low CPI inflation and similar FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in nonfarm payroll employment signals immediate deterioration in wage income and corporate operating leverage, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 137615 thousand persons as of July 2008
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS monthly declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their average return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Weak real GDP growth together with falling industrial production confirms a broad demand-supply recession signal, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth is below 1% while INDPRO 3-month growth is negative
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 11700.6 as of 2008Q1
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weak-GDPC1 and negative-INDPRO condition is followed by above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than half of triggered walk-forward observations.
